hard.next_step: 6.29.26 KK - had commercial call. Pricing well received. They understand the timeline is tight and they need to make a decision in the coming week or so. RISK- coupa is going onsite wednesday. sharing commercials with value slides on why zycus for platform and partnership offering executive session a Aatish / Bikash 6/22mc- Followed up with Bass Pro to re-engage David and Whitney on the updated proposal. Left David a voicemail and also sent Whitney a text requesting 20-30 minutes with both of them to review the proposal, confirm alignment on scope/pricing, and define next steps. Current risk is increasing as the account has been unresponsive for over a week, despite the proposal being ready for review. Strategy is to use the pending customer reference conversations as a reason to re-engage David and create urgency around the evaluation. If no response is received by Wednesday, June 24, we will escalate thoughtfully by expanding outreach to additional executive stakeholders, including the CFO and CPO/David's boss, to request an executive alignment meeting. To date, we have held back on broader executive outreach because David indicated the process should move under his direction, but lack of engagement may require us to create additional visibility and momentum to protect the deal.
hard.ais_why: AI importance is logged as 'Neutral' and the agentic-platform discussion, Gen AI commentary and AI-RFP rating fields are all blank. The Merlin product line tagging is Zycus-side rather than a documented buyer ask; across the March-April meetings the buyer's action items were strictly pricing and P2P scoping, with no buyer-voiced AI signal.

forecast_read.reason: Upside Key Deal is honest for a buyer-voiced final-two against the incumbent with pricing well received on 29 Jun and a decision promised within about a week. The exposure is the date, not the category: even a mid-July verbal leaves MSA, SOW and InfoSec untouched with 21 days to the 30 Jul close.
forecast_read.math: 21 days to close as of 2026-07-09; in Shortlisted 43 days (Shortlisted_Submission via stage move 2026-05-27); close pushed once 2026-06-30 to 2026-07-30 on 29 May; decision promised 'in the coming week or so' on 29 Jun means a verbal is due now, then the entire contracting relay must compress into roughly two weeks to hold 30 Jul.

metrics.narrative: The deal is sized, not value-quantified. The buyer corrected the commercial baseline on the 6 Apr proposal review: David Elford stated total spend is about $1.4B for analytics while 'only $250 [million] of that roughly runs through P2P', plus 1,500 procurement and intake users, roughly 520 real contracts a year and 225,000 invoices. But no buyer-voiced ROI or savings number exists; David's own bar on 6 Apr was 'make sure the math makes sense to undertake a conversion', and the Zycus ROI framing sent 29 Jun is our material, not his confirmation.
sources: Avoma transcript, Zycus - High Level Proposal Review, 2026-04-06
Email, Bass Pro x Zycus Proposal, Zycus ROI & Next Steps, 2026-06-29

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is mapped or engaged. David Elford (Director, Non-Merchandise Procurement) directs the evaluation and has told the team 'the process should move under his direction' (Next Step, 22 Jun), but who signs off a five-year, roughly $445k-per-year commitment above him is unknown: the CFO and the CPO (David's boss) are named only as escalation targets we have deliberately not yet approached. No budget-approval conversation is on record in any call or field.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are buyer-voiced and specific. On 27 May David set the bar: preserve current T&E functionality and the Egencia booking experience ('You've convinced me. It's very similar to what we have today'), fix spend visibility ('the data is there... accessing it is not easy'), integrate legacy payables ledgers (Infinium, KBM, IDS on AS400, plus Timberline and Workday), and prove downstream P2P usability for 2,000 users, backed by reference calls with former Coupa customers. On 6 Apr he added single intake as 'a big improvement' and pushed to full-suite scope with 5-year pricing.
sources: Avoma transcript, Demo Meeting with Zycus - Intro to Emburse, 2026-05-27

decision_process.narrative: It is an informal evaluation, not an RFP: David confirmed on 27 May 'this is part of that evaluation' and 'at this point, it's pretty much down to you and the incumbent.' The forcing function is the Coupa contract expiring February 2027 after a one-year extension taken 'to give us a chance to look at alternatives', and our 6-7 month implementation math led David to say 'by the end of June, we need to make a decision'; on 29 Jun he moved that to 'the coming week or so.' What happens after David decides, board or finance approval, is not established.

paper_process.narrative: Commercial constructs are agreed in principle but no paper has started. On 6 Apr the buyer accepted the 1.1-1.3x implementation-fee rule of thumb, asked for 5-year pricing, and Alexa Bradley offered milestone payments ('can be very flexible on the milestone payments'); David confirmed implementation would be amortized as fixed-fee milestones. No MSA, Order Form, SOW, legal or InfoSec track exists yet, which is the main threat to the 30 Jul close date even on a won decision.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed in the buyer's own words. David on 27 May: Coupa 'changed their data visualization engine two, maybe 3 years ago, and instead of being a step forward, it was a step backwards', and 'the data is there, but accessing it is not easy.' Whitney confirmed our pain recap 'looks pretty much like what we've explained to you' (27 May), and on 6 Apr David called single intake functionality 'we don't have today... that would be a big improvement.' The pain owner is the procurement leadership itself, which is why the evaluation exists.

champion.narrative: Whitney Parks (Non Merchandise Procurement Manager) is a developing champion: she coordinates every session, supplied the T&E volume data, and when asked which sandbox she wanted answered 'Zycus for sure, but Emburse wouldn't hurt' (29 May). What developed since is real access, she brokered the 29 Jun review; what is missing is proof she advocates upward, she paused the sandbox on 2 Jun pending the numbers, and she does not control the decision, David does. David is engaged and constructive but keeps the Coupa option genuinely open, so he is the decision maker, not a champion.

competition.narrative: This is a two-horse race against the incumbent. David, 27 May: 'it's pretty much down to you and the incumbent. So it's just the two that we're seriously considering.' Coupa's contract expires February 2027, Bass Pro 'has invested significantly in Coupa over the years' (our 30 Jun email acknowledging the buyer's position), and Coupa ran an onsite with Bass Pro on Wednesday 1 Jul (Next Step, 29 Jun) while the buyer has been quiet with us since the 29 Jun review. Coupa's incumbency also earns buyer credit on features: David noted on 27 May that receipt-photo capture is 'one of the things that Coupa brought us.'

critical_signals.text: Coupa, the incumbent whose contract runs to February 2027, took an onsite with Bass Pro on 1 Jul, and the buyer has gone quiet with us through the very week David Elford promised a decision.
At the 29 Jun proposal review David Elford, Whitney Parks and Chris Rodger received the revised full-suite pricing well and David committed to a decision 'in the coming week or so'.
David's stated gate before deciding, reference calls with former Coupa P2P customers to settle his downstream concern for 2,000 users, has been open since 27 May and is still undelivered.
The full-suite offer was repriced from $556.5k to $445.2k on 12 Jun to make the switch-from-Coupa math work, and the buyer has not yet said yes to it.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A genuine final-two against incumbent Coupa, decided by David Elford in the next days: pricing was well received on 29 Jun and the Feb 2027 Coupa expiry forces a decision now, but the buyer has been silent for 10 days while Coupa ran a 1 Jul onsite, no economic buyer above David is engaged, and David's own reference-call gate is unmet.

win_position.text: Buyer-voiced final two: David Elford said on 27 May 'it's pretty much down to you and the incumbent', putting Zycus one decision away from displacing Coupa.
The revised $445.2k full-suite proposal was 'well received' at the 29 Jun review and David committed to deciding 'in the coming week or so' (Next Step, 29 Jun).
A real compelling event: the Coupa contract expires February 2027 and David accepted on 27 May that the 6-7 month implementation means 'we got to get going soon'.
Coupa is the incumbent Bass Pro 'has invested significantly in' and it ran an onsite on 1 Jul while our threads (30 Jun, 7 Jul) sit unanswered, so the last voice in the room is the competitor's.
No economic buyer is engaged: budget approval above David (CFO, CPO) is unmapped and untouched because David asked to run the process himself (Next Step, 22 Jun).
David's decision gate, ex-Coupa P2P reference calls he asked for on 27 May to settle his 2,000-user downstream concern, has not been delivered before his decision window.

deal_momentum.text: The buyer drove the last meeting: David proposed the 29 Jun 3:00 pm slot himself on 25 Jun and attended the proposal review with Whitney and Chris.
Commercial topics dominate: a 12 Jun reprice to $445.2k followed by a full proposal review on 29 Jun with pricing well received.
Ten days of buyer silence since 29 Jun, through the promised decision week, with the 30 Jun and 7 Jul check-ins unanswered.
Nothing is on the calendar: the executive session with Aatish and Bikash is offered in the Next Step but no dated invite exists.

customer_commitment.text: David committed on 29 Jun to a decision 'in the coming week or so' and on 27 May conceded the timeline math, 'by the end of June, we need to make a decision'.
Whitney asked for a Zycus sandbox on 29 May ('Zycus for sure') and supplied the T&E volume data twice, real buyer effort invested in our option.
The sandbox was parked by Whitney on 2 Jun pending the numbers and has not been re-opened since the proposal landed.

deal_risk.text: Incumbent counter-attack at the decision point: Coupa onsite 1 Jul plus buyer silence since 29 Jun is the classic shape of a renewal being re-sold.
Single point of power: everything routes through David Elford, and if he goes quiet the deal has no other lever, the CFO/CPO escalation planned on 22 Jun was never executed.
Even a won decision this month strains the 30 Jul close: no MSA, SOW or InfoSec track has started (6 Apr was the last paper-process conversation).

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer-voiced final two on 27 May (David Elford: 'it's pretty much down to you and the incumbent... just the two that we're seriously considering') and 'You've convinced me' on T&E parity; but no buyer statement that Zycus is ahead of Coupa, and 'pricing well received' on 29 Jun is the rep's read.

business_case.evidence: Commercial baseline is buyer-corrected ($250M through P2P, 1,500 users, 225k invoices, 6 Apr) and Zycus ROI material was walked through on 29 Jun, but the buyer has not confirmed a value case; David's test on 6 Apr was 'make sure the math makes sense to undertake a conversion'.

deal_movement.summary: The deal advanced to a commercial decision point: repriced 12 Jun, proposal reviewed 29 Jun with a decision promised within a week, ownership moved to Karson Keogh 26 Jun, and Coupa countered with a 1 Jul onsite.

items.change: Proposal review held with David Elford, Whitney Parks and Chris Rodger; pricing well received and a decision promised 'in the coming week or so'
Opportunity owner changed from Mario Castro to Karson Keogh by Alexa Bradley
Amount reduced from $556,500 to $445,200 on the updated full-suite proposal
Stage advanced Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted and forecast moved Pipeline to Best Case to Upside Key Deal; same day David voiced the down-select to a final two vs the incumbent

day_summary.overall: The last day with real deal activity was 29 Jun: David Elford, Whitney Parks and Chris Rodger sat the proposal review that David himself scheduled, the revised $445.2k full-suite pricing was well received, and David committed to a decision in the coming week or so, while flagging that Coupa would be onsite that Wednesday. Since then the only touches are Zycus check-ins on 30 Jun and 7 Jul, neither answered.

items.name: Proposal review with Bass Pro
items.summary: Revised full-suite commercials and ROI walked through with David, Whitney and Chris; pricing well received, decision promised within about a week, Coupa onsite flagged for 1 Jul.

items.name: Post-review recap to David, Chris and Whitney
items.summary: Thanked the team, recapped the proposal and platform ROI discussion, and set up next steps.

competitive_position.summary: A buyer-confirmed two-horse race: Zycus versus incumbent Coupa, whose contract expires February 2027 after a one-year extension taken specifically to evaluate alternatives. Coupa holds incumbency, heavy sunk investment and feature credit with the buyer, and it ran an onsite on 1 Jul during the decision week while the buyer went quiet with us. We win on the buyer's own criteria: spend visibility (David called Coupa's analytics 'a step backwards'), single intake Coupa does not give them, T&E parity already conceded ('You've convinced me'), and the switch economics of the repriced 5-year full suite.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: One of the things that Coupa brought us, we didn't have before... is the ability to take a picture of that invoice and upload that information.
competitors.source: Avoma transcript 2026-05-27 (quote); Next_Step__c 2026-06-29 ('RISK- coupa is going onsite wednesday'); Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Press the buyer's own pain, Coupa's analytics regression and missing single intake, land the ex-Coupa P2P references David asked for, and make the Feb 2027 expiry plus 6-7 month implementation math force the decision before Coupa re-sells the renewal onsite.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus is aligned or ahead on analytics, intake and T&E parity; the exposed ground is downstream P2P proof for 2,000 users and the unproven legacy-ledger integrations, exactly where David says his nerves are.

items.criterion: Preserve current T&E functionality and Egencia booking experience
items.quote: You've convinced me. It's very similar to what we have today. So it's not like we'd be sacrificing any functionality.

items.criterion: Spend visibility and one reporting engine across procurement and expense
items.quote: You're hitting the nail on the head... to be able to get the data and do [something] with it.
That's functionality we don't have today... that would be a big improvement.

items.criterion: Integrations to legacy AS400 payables ledgers (Infinium, KBM, IDS), Timberline and Workday
items.quote: A lot of antiquated technology... that is where we have to build integrations. That's probably where my primary concern is.
items.source: Avoma transcript, David Elford; iSaaS Advance 80/20 model presented in response

items.criterion: Downstream P2P usability and change management for 2,000 users
items.quote: Upstream touches round numbers 20 people. Downstream touches 2,000 people... that's where my primary concern is.
items.source: Avoma transcript, David Elford; requested ex-Coupa references still pending

items.requirement: Reference calls with Zycus P2P customers who previously ran Coupa, covering the tool, transition and change management
items.quote: What would be ideal would be if you have customers who use P2P that were previous Coupa customers... their thoughts on your tool, also the transition... and what change management was involved.
items.source: Avoma transcript, 2026-05-27; still 'pending customer reference conversations' in Next_Step__c 22 Jun

items.requirement: Updated commercial proposal encompassing Zycus plus Emburse

items.source: Avoma transcript, 2026-05-27; delivered 10 Jun and reviewed 29 Jun

items.requirement: 5-year full-suite price with module one-pagers and license-count breakdown

items.source: Avoma transcript, 2026-04-06; one-pagers sent on-call, pricing updated 12 Jun

items.requirement: Horizon agenda plus access to last year's session recordings

items.source: Avoma transcript, 2026-05-27; no evidence of delivery in any later activity

best_practice_check.summary: The deal is at its decision point with three levers unpulled: the incumbent's onsite went unanswered, power above David is untouched, and David's own reference gate is unmet.
flags: Counter the incumbent's 1 Jul onsite: secure a live debrief with David this week so Coupa's renewal pitch is not the last word before the decision.
Break the single point of power: execute the exec-alignment plan already written on 22 Jun, Alexa Bradley to the CFO and CPO (David's boss), if David stays silent past 13 Jul.
Close David's stated deal-killer, downstream P2P doubt for 2,000 users, by delivering both ex-Coupa reference calls plus a change-management proof pack before he decides.

champion_strength.summary: Whitney Parks is a developing champion: she voiced a Zycus preference on the sandbox ('Zycus for sure', 29 May), supplies data, and brokered the 29 Jun review, but the decision sits with David Elford and her upward advocacy is unproven.
champion_strength.source: Email RE: Sandbox? 2026-05-29; email thread 2026-06-25; Avoma transcripts 2026-04-06 and 2026-05-27

scope_change.from: P2P-core options (downstream plus spend analytics bundles)
scope_change.to: Full S2P suite (less iSave and iManage) on a 5-year term, repriced from $556.5k to $445.2k on 12 Jun
scope_change.detail: On 6 Apr David chose the full platform himself ('For 100,000 more, I think it would be foolish of us not to consider going all the way') and asked for 5-year pricing; the 12 Jun amount reduction is a price move on the same full-suite scope, not a scope cut.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is not a buying criterion here: the buyer's AI posture is logged Neutral, no AI ask appears in any call, and the evaluation runs on P2P usability, spend visibility, integrations and price. Leading with Merlin or Agentic AI would not move this buyer.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 3.0 Negative: across roughly three hours of recorded discussion (12 Mar to 6 Apr) and both later transcripts, the buyer never raised AI, autonomous negotiation or Merlin; their asks are pricing, P2P scoping and integrations.

items.detail: Decision promised 'in the coming week or so' on 29 Jun has not landed, and even a mid-July verbal leaves MSA, SOW and InfoSec to run inside 21 days to the 30 Jul close.
Incumbent counter-move: Coupa onsite on 1 Jul during the decision week, against a buyer that 'has invested significantly in Coupa over the years', with our threads unanswered since.
No access above David Elford: the CFO and CPO are unengaged because David asked to run the process under his direction, leaving the deal single-threaded at the power level.
David's precondition, ex-Coupa P2P reference calls, has been open since 27 May and is still pending at the decision point.
David's core doubt is downstream: 'Upstream touches 20 people. Downstream touches 2,000 people', covering supplier enablement through user adoption on an unfamiliar tool.

Three pre-ERP AS400 payables ledgers (Infinium, KBM, IDS) plus Timberline and Workday must integrate; David called this 'probably where my primary concern is' and Bass Pro IT (Kelean Carlock) has not been engaged.

confidence_signals.summary: The deal ran hot into 29 Jun, buyer-scheduled review, pricing well received, but has cooled since: 10 days of buyer silence through the promised decision week, a repeat of the mid-June unresponsive stretch, this time with Coupa freshly onsite.
items: No reply to the 30 Jun and 7 Jul follow-up emails after the 29 Jun review
Earlier pattern: 'unresponsive for over a week' logged 22 Jun before David re-engaged on 25 Jun
Sandbox request paused by Whitney on 2 Jun and never re-opened after the proposal landed

drivers: ✅ Buyer-voiced final two: David Elford said on 27 May 'it's pretty much down to you and the incumbent', so Zycus is one decision from displacing Coupa.
✅ The repriced $445.2k full-suite proposal was well received at the 29 Jun review David scheduled himself, and he committed to a decision 'in the coming week or so'.
✅ A real compelling event: Coupa's contract expires February 2027 and David accepted that the 6-7 month implementation means 'we got to get going soon'.

⚠️ Coupa is a deeply invested incumbent and ran an onsite on 1 Jul while our 30 Jun and 7 Jul threads sit unanswered ► call David this week for a decision readout and onsite debrief before Coupa's pitch settles.
⚠️ No economic buyer is engaged: the CFO and CPO above David are unmapped and untouched ► use the offered Aatish/Bikash executive session to open a power-level thread now.
⚠️ David's own gate, ex-Coupa P2P reference calls requested 27 May, is still undelivered at the decision point ► land both prepped references by 14 Jul.
✅ The buyer drove the last engagement: David proposed the 29 Jun 3:00 pm slot on 25 Jun and attended the proposal review with Whitney Parks and Chris Rodger.
✅ Commercial motion is live: a 12 Jun reprice to $445.2k followed by a full proposal review on 29 Jun with pricing well received.
⚠️ Ten days of buyer silence since 29 Jun, through the promised decision week, with two check-ins unanswered ► pick up the phone to David rather than send a third email.
⚠️ Nothing is booked forward: the executive session is offered, not dated ► convert it to a calendar invite this week.
⚠️ The freshest buyer-side activity belongs to the competitor (Coupa onsite, 1 Jul) ► secure a post-onsite debrief to reclaim the conversation.
✅ Close date has held at 30 Jul since a single 29 May push, consistent with the decide-now timeline.

sum.headline: No change in the last 24h: Bass Pro has not replied since the 29 Jun proposal review, where pricing was well received and a decision was promised within the week.
